panels.description: Wide panoramic shot of a Victorian coastal churchyard overlooking the sea. A funeral procession carries a coffin up from the pier. In the foreground, two young women (Lucy and narrator) watch solemnly from an old wooden seat near a fresh grave. Somber, atmospheric, coastal light.

bubbles.text: The funeral of the sea-captain was truly touching today. Lucy and I watched as they laid him to rest right beside our old seat.

panels.description: Medium shot of Lucy looking pale and unsettled. In the background, a small crowd gathers around the spot where Mr. Swales was found, a doctor gesturing. The expression on the late Mr. Swales's face (implied by onlookers' reactions) is one of pure terror.

bubbles.text: Lucy is so restless. And this morning... poor Mr. Swales was found dead on this very seat, his face frozen in such horror.

panels.description: Medium close-up of a dog snarling, hairs bristling, eyes wild with unexplainable fury. Its master stands nearby, looking confused and trying to call it back.
The man drags the dog onto the tombstone base. Instantly, the dog changes from a state of fury to a shivering, cowering, pitiable state of pure terror. High contrast lighting.
Close-up of Lucy's face, looking deeply distressed and sensitive, eyes wide with empathy and hidden fear. The narrator places a hand gently on her shoulder.

bubbles.text: I fear her nature is too sensitive for this world. She will surely dream of this terror tonight.

panels.description: Wide shot of the two women walking away towards the cliffs, back to the camera. The sea air is blowing. They are heading towards the distant horizon.

bubbles.text: We must walk until she is exhausted. It is the only way to keep the nightmares at bay.
